chapter: Chapter 2: The Invisible Living World: Beyond Our Naked Eye

questions.question: Why can't many microorganisms be seen with the naked eye?
questions.explanation: Microorganisms are extremely small and require microscopes to be seen.

questions.question: Which instrument is used to observe cells and microorganisms?
questions.explanation: Microscopes magnify very small objects like cells and microorganisms.

questions.question: Who discovered cells by observing cork under a microscope?
questions.explanation: Robert Hooke observed cork and named the small compartments cells.

questions.question: Who is known as the Father of Microbiology?
questions.explanation: Leeuwenhoek first observed microorganisms using improved microscopes.

questions.question: Which part of the cell controls its activities?
questions.explanation: The nucleus acts as the control center of the cell.

questions.question: Which structure surrounds and protects the cell?
questions.explanation: The cell membrane encloses the cell and regulates movement of substances.

questions.question: Which cell structure is present in plant cells but not in animal cells?
questions.explanation: Plant cells have a cell wall which provides support and protection.

questions.question: Which microorganisms are responsible for curd formation?
questions.explanation: Lactobacillus bacteria convert milk into curd.

questions.question: Which microorganism is commonly used in bread making?
questions.explanation: Yeast ferments sugar and releases carbon dioxide which makes bread rise.

questions.question: Which process helps microorganisms break down dead plants and animals?
questions.explanation: Microorganisms decompose organic matter into simpler substances.

questions.question: Which bacteria live in root nodules of leguminous plants?
questions.explanation: Rhizobium bacteria fix nitrogen in the soil.

questions.question: Which microorganisms produce oxygen through photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: Microalgae perform photosynthesis and release oxygen.

questions.question: Which group of microorganisms includes Amoeba?
questions.explanation: Amoeba is a protozoan microorganism.

questions.question: Which level of organization comes after tissues?
questions.explanation: Tissues combine to form organs.

questions.question: Which of the following is a unicellular organism?
questions.explanation: Amoeba consists of a single cell.

questions.question: Which part of the cell contains genetic material?
questions.explanation: The nucleus contains genetic material that controls cell functions.

questions.question: Which microorganisms help convert organic waste into manure?
questions.explanation: Decomposer microorganisms break down organic waste into compost.

questions.question: What gas is produced during yeast fermentation?
questions.explanation: Carbon dioxide produced by yeast makes dough rise.

questions.question: Which scientific tool is most important for studying cells?
questions.explanation: Cells are observed using microscopes.

questions.question: Which of the following organisms performs photosynthesis and lives in water?
questions.explanation: Microalgae perform photosynthesis in aquatic environments.

questions.question: What is the basic unit of life?
questions.explanation: Cells are the fundamental units of all living organisms.

questions.question: Which microorganisms can cause diseases?
questions.explanation: Some microorganisms cause diseases in humans, animals, and plants.

questions.question: Which microorganism is commonly found in pond water?
questions.explanation: Amoeba is a common protozoan found in pond water.

questions.question: Which process helps recycle nutrients in ecosystems?
questions.explanation: Decomposition by microorganisms returns nutrients to the soil.

questions.question: Why are microorganisms important for the environment?

options.B: They recycle nutrients and support ecosystems

questions.explanation: Microorganisms help maintain ecological balance by recycling nutrients.
